summary: Most external deps are well-researched and have explicit fallbacks (OpenRouter primary + direct-OpenAI fallback, Langfuse Cloud + local trace fallback, pgvector + app-level cosine fallback, Neo4j deferred-with-spike, React Flow). The research is honest about three remaining-risk items that are tracked as open questions: exact OpenRouter model routes (cost/latency spike), cost ceiling/rate limits, and Codex feasibility. Each of those has a sensible fallback, so they are 'covered by a spike' rather than hard blockers. The one genuinely UNRESEARCHED, UNFLAGGED external dependency is the retrieval / prior-art search source for critic grounding — CONSTRAINTS.md explicitly flagged it as [research required], but no RQ in RESEARCH.md addresses it, and it is load-bearing for the zeitgeist 'current signals' subtype and for any grounding/falsifiability check. That is the primary blocker. Two account-access items (OpenRouter project rate limits/account, Langfuse Cloud account) are verifiable-but-unverified and should be confirmed before they sit on the demo critical path.

findings.finding: Retrieval / prior-art search source for critic grounding is unresearched and has no RQ, no spike, and no fallback — despite being explicitly flagged as research-required. The zeitgeist_synthesis subtype is premised on 'current signals' data and grounding/novelty/falsifiability checks depend on some external evidence source; without a decided source (web search API, curated corpus, or model-native tool), the verifier council cannot ground claims and the zeitgeist subtype's checks degrade to subjective critic opinion.
findings.evidence: CONSTRAINTS.md §External Dependencies line 29 ('Any retrieval / prior-art search dependency needs research if critic grounding relies on web or corpus search'); RESEARCH.md RQ table (RQ-001..RQ-010) contains no retrieval/prior-art RQ; OPEN_QUESTIONS.md OQ-004 ('zeitgeist gets grounding/novelty/falsifiability checks') assumes grounding with no named source.
findings.proposed_fix: Add an RQ + a short spike that picks a single MVP grounding source and an explicit fallback. Recommended default: defer live web retrieval; ground critics against an operator-curated static prior-art/signals corpus seeded per prepared demo prompt (OQ-001/OQ-012), and record 'no live retrieval' as an MVP deferral. If a live source is wanted, evaluate one provider-native/web-search tool behind the ModelGateway, but treat its extra cost (RESEARCH.md R-002 notes web-search tool calls have extra cost) and latency as demo risk.

findings.finding: OpenRouter project-level rate limits and account access are listed as remaining risk but not verified; OpenRouter is the LOCKED primary provider path and a population-scale loop (~20 agenomes x critics x checks x 2-4 generations) can burst many calls inside the 10-minute live window, where per-route rate limits could throttle the demo.
findings.evidence: RESEARCH.md R-001 Remaining Risk ('Project-level rate limits and account access are not known') and R-009 Remaining Risk ('OpenRouter pricing/rate limits/model availability can vary by routed model and must be checked during provider spike'); CONSTRAINTS.md Runtime Budgets line 91 ([research required] rate limits/concurrency); locked primary path in DECISIONS.md ADR-004.
findings.proposed_fix: Fold rate-limit/account verification into the provider cost/latency spike that already gates OQ-005/OQ-006. Provision the OpenRouter account and run one full-population prototype to measure concurrency headroom. Fallback already exists and is locked (ADR-004: switch primary to direct OpenAI if OpenRouter rate limits block the demo) — make that switch a rehearsed runbook step, not a live scramble.

findings.finding: Embeddings route ambiguity is partly resolved but the operative dependency is unstated: OpenRouter is the locked primary path, yet embeddings are repeatedly assigned to direct OpenAI, so the embeddings call bypasses the primary route. Whether OpenRouter can serve the chosen embedding model (and at what cost/limit) is not researched, leaving a hidden hard dependency on a direct-OpenAI account even in the 'OpenRouter-only if time is tight' fallback.
findings.evidence: RESEARCH.md R-003 (OpenAI embeddings + pgvector) and Research Summary ('First direct provider adapter/fallback: OpenAI with structured outputs and embeddings'); DECISIONS.md ADR-004 Decision ('OpenAI remains a direct adapter/fallback, especially for embeddings'); ADR-004 Fallback ('Ship with OpenRouter only ... add direct OpenAI fallback if OpenRouter capability gaps block ... embeddings').
findings.proposed_fix: Lock embeddings explicitly to direct OpenAI behind the ModelGateway as a deliberate MVP decision (record it), so it is not treated as a deferrable fallback. Provision the OpenAI API key in the same spike. Recommended default model: text-embedding-3-small (RESEARCH.md R-003). Document that the 'OpenRouter-only' fallback still requires an OpenAI key for embeddings, or specify the app-level cosine path that avoids embeddings entirely if no OpenAI key is available.

findings.finding: Cost ceiling / token budget is unresolved and has no numeric guardrail yet; the energy/cost caps that are load-bearing safety rails depend on numbers that 'require a prototype run' but the ceiling itself is still open, so caps cannot be configured to a real budget before the spike.
findings.evidence: RESEARCH.md R-002 Remaining Risk ('Exact cost ceiling still needs the team's budget and provider account limits. Token estimates require a prototype run'); RQ table status RQ-002 ('researched, cost ceiling still open'); CONSTRAINTS.md Runtime Budgets line 91 ([research required] exact cost ceiling); OPEN_QUESTIONS.md OQ-013 / Phase-0 'population/generation/token/time defaults ... After provider research'.
findings.proposed_fix: Treat as question-for-human-adjacent but spike-resolvable: run one prototype population, measure tokens/run, set a hard per-run cost cap in the energy ledger before the first end-to-end run. Recommended default until measured: tier population/critic calls onto the cheaper model (gpt-5.4-mini class per R-002) and reserve the flagship only for final judge/synthesis, with a conservative lower live-demo override (CONSTRAINTS.md line 87).

findings.finding: Langfuse Cloud account access is verifiable-but-unverified. The dependency is non-authoritative and already has a strong local-trace fallback, so it is not blocking, but it should be confirmed early since it is locked as the MVP observability platform.
findings.evidence: RESEARCH.md R-004 Remaining Risk ('Langfuse Cloud availability and account setup must be verified') and Impact ('Doppl's own event log must still preserve enough trace-like metadata'); DECISIONS.md ADR-005 Fallback ('Store minimal local trace metadata in Postgres').
findings.proposed_fix: Create the Langfuse Cloud account and confirm API keys during setup. No spike needed beyond that — the local trace-metadata fallback (ADR-005) already covers a Langfuse outage, so this is purely an account-provisioning checklist item, not a build blocker.

findings.finding: pgvector setup is researched with a clear app-level-cosine fallback, so it is not a blocker, but the trigger that decides pgvector-vs-app-cosine is still an open question rather than a spike with a deadline — leaving the team without a crisp go/no-go gate.
findings.evidence: RESEARCH.md R-003/R-007 (pgvector-node researched, app-level cosine fallback) and ADR-006 Fallback; OPEN_QUESTIONS.md line 40 ('Should MVP novelty scoring use pgvector from day one, or start with app-level cosine ... and migrate') and OQ-007.
findings.proposed_fix: Set a timeboxed pgvector enablement spike (e.g., 'CREATE EXTENSION vector' + one nearest-neighbor query working within half a day). Recommended default: start with app-level cosine over the small candidate set (MVP scale is tiny) and keep the storage contract pgvector-ready, promoting only if the simple path proves limiting. This matches the locked fallback and removes the open question.

findings.finding: Codex feasibility is correctly de-risked: it is explicitly NOT a must-ship runtime provider and is gated behind separate validation. It remains an open question but cannot block the build because nothing depends on it.
findings.evidence: RESEARCH.md R-009 Remaining Risk ('Codex subscription access needs separate feasibility validation and should not be assumed as an API model provider'); DECISIONS.md ADR-004 ([research required] Codex) and Phase-12 table row 'Codex subscription access'; OPEN_QUESTIONS.md Phase-0 Codex question.
findings.proposed_fix: No action required for the build to proceed; keep Codex strictly research-only per the locked baseline. If validated later, add it as an operator/execution adapter behind the ModelGateway, never as a runtime model dependency. Confirm the implementation plan does not place any task on the Codex critical path.

findings.finding: Decision needed: what grounding/'current signals' data source is acceptable for the zeitgeist_synthesis subtype in the showcase — live web retrieval, a curated static corpus, or no external grounding (critic-only)? This determines whether the zeitgeist subtype's checks are credible and is the unblock for the critical retrieval finding above.
findings.evidence: CONSTRAINTS.md line 29 ([research required] retrieval/prior-art); OPEN_QUESTIONS.md OQ-004 (zeitgeist grounding/novelty/falsifiability checks) and OQ-001/OQ-012 (prepared vs live prompt); LOCKED baseline canonical unit = zeitgeist_synthesis subtype.
findings.proposed_fix: Recommended default: for the MVP/demo, use an operator-curated static prior-art/'signals' corpus tied to the prepared demo prompt(s), and explicitly defer live web retrieval as stretch. This keeps the zeitgeist checks grounded against fixed evidence, avoids an unbudgeted external search dependency and its cost/latency/rate-limit surface, and keeps secrets/data out of an uncontrolled web path — consistent with local-first reliability and the prepared-prompt fallback.
